reasoning: As of early May 2025, no official date for a Scottish independence referendum has been announced by the Scottish Government. Since the 2022 UK Supreme Court ruling that the Scottish Parliament lacks the unilateral power to hold a referendum, the political focus of the Scottish National Party (SNP) has shifted. Following significant seat losses in the July 2024 UK General Election, First Minister John Swinney has pivoted toward 'building the case' for independence and focusing on the upcoming 2026 Scottish Parliament elections as the next mandate. News reports and official statements as late as April 2025 consistently point to 2026 as the next significant milestone, with no immediate plans to schedule a referendum in the current term. Given the lack of a date announcement with only two months remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline, and no legislative or political momentum suggesting such an announcement is imminent, the probability is extremely low.
